no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly states the exact implementation: 'An RPC method named snapshot on the worker that writes the server heap to server.heapsnapshot', 'An onSnapshot callback in the TUI thread that triggers both snapshot writes (TUI locally, server via RPC) and returns both file paths', 'The onSnapshot callback must be passed as an optional prop through to the App component'. This is prescriptive of WHAT to change rather than just describing what's broken.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check for exact string matches like 'snapshot()' in worker.ts, 'onSnapshot?:' in app.tsx, 'onSnapshot={input.onSnapshot}' in app.tsx, and 'client.call("snapshot"' in thread.ts. An alternative correct implementation using e.g. 'heapSnapshot' as the method name, or destructuring the prop differently (e.g., `onSnapshot={props.onSnapshot}`), or using a different RPC calling pattern would fail these text-matching tests.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The four fail-to-pass tests (test_worker_has_snapshot_rpc, test_thread_implements_onsnapshot, test_app_accepts_onsnapshot_prop, test_changelog_command_updated) all exclusively read file contents as strings and check for substring/regex matches. None of them import, execute, or call the code to verify actual behavior. They are pure text-matching tests.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub implementation that simply adds the right string literals to each file (e.g., adding dead-code comments containing 'writeHeapSnapshot', 'snapshot()', 'server.heapsnapshot', 'onSnapshot?:', 'client.call("snapshot"', etc.) would pass all four fail-to-pass tests without implementing any actual functionality, since the tests only check for string presence in file contents.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: The changelog.md edit is localized (restructuring content of one file) and doesn't contradict other files. The code edits are also localized additions to existing files.
